violations.description: Email/username and password inputs lack autocomplete attributes. WCAG 1.3.5 requires identifying the purpose of inputs that collect user information so browsers and assistive technologies can autofill them.
violations.reference_fix:
<input type="text" placeholder="Enter your email or username" id="email" autocomplete="username">
<input type="password" placeholder="Enter your password" id="password" autocomplete="current-password">

violations.description: The visual CAPTCHA displays distorted text with no audio or alternative mechanism for users who cannot see the image. This blocks blind users from completing the login flow entirely.
violations.reference_fix:
Security verification

X7kQ9m

violations.description: Text inputs for email, password, and CAPTCHA use <span> elements or placeholder text instead of programmatically associated <label> elements. Screen readers cannot identify the purpose of these fields.
violations.reference_fix:
<label for="email">Email or Username</label>
<input type="text" id="email" placeholder="Enter your email or username">

violations.description: Social login buttons contain only decorative colored spans with no text content, aria-label, or aria-labelledby. Screen readers announce them as empty buttons with no indication of their purpose.
Password hint text uses color #ccc on a white background, yielding a contrast ratio of approximately 1.6:1, far below the 4.5:1 minimum required for normal text.
violations.reference_fix: Must be at least 8 characters

page_description: A banking login page with email and password fields, a visual CAPTCHA challenge, social login buttons for Google, Apple, and Microsoft, and a password recovery link. Issues include missing autocomplete attributes, an image CAPTCHA with no audio alternative, inputs lacking programmatic labels, social buttons with no accessible names, and low-contrast hint text.
